# Project Guidelines

## Overview
This repository is a personal portfolio. Primary entry: `index` at the repository root. The workspace currently contains minimal files; keep instructions concise and actionable.

## Build and Test
- There are no known build or test commands in this repository at the moment.
- If a framework or toolchain is added, add installation/build/test commands here (e.g., `npm install`, `npm run build`, `npm test`).

## Conventions
- Edit `index` for site content and structure.
- Prefer relative asset paths so the site works when deployed as static files.
- Use small, focused commits with imperative messages (e.g., "Add projects section").

## Example Agent Prompts
- "Improve the hero section in `index` to be more concise and action-oriented."
- "Add a responsive navigation bar to `index` with accessible keyboard support."
- "Create a Contact section in `index` with basic client-side validation."

## Suggested Next Customizations
- Add a `./.github/prompts/` folder with reusable prompts for common tasks (site updates, content edits).
- If this repository grows into multiple packages, consider `AGENTS.md` files per subfolder instead of a single workspace file.
- Add user-level prompts in the VS Code prompts folder for personal shortcuts (see user settings sync).
